message.content:

Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "claim with negative damage amount causes error"
Current error: AssertionError: expected [Function] to throw an error
Passing tests so far: 23

The claim function must throw when any damage entry has a negative amount (e.g., amount: -200).

Implement minimal validation: throw a clear Error when any damage.amount < 0.

All 23 previously passing tests must still pass. Do NOT refactor.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal validation to make the failing test pass. Let me first read the current state of the implementation and test files.
Now I'll add the minimal validation - throw an error when any damage amount is negative.

content.text:
All 24 tests pass.

🟢 Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added a minimal validation loop at the start of `claim` that throws an `Error` when any damage entry has a negative amount.
**Result**: All tests now pass (24 passing, up from 23).
**Approach**: This is minimal because it only adds the smallest piece of logic the failing test demands — a direct check on `damage.amount < 0` that throws when violated. No refactoring of existing structure was performed; no additional validations (e.g. zero amounts, non-numeric amounts) were added since the test does not demand them.
